original_thesis: How information gets compressed, transmitted, and distorted. Abstraction and simplification compress reality into transmittable form; signaling carries it; narratives make it sticky. First-principles thinking rebuilds from scratch when compression has distorted. Anchoring is the failure mode — when simplification oversimplifies and the compressed signal becomes the anchor.
what_this_stack_defeats: Miscommunication, lossy abstraction, signal degradation across organizational layers
notes: Thesis mentions anchoring as failure mode but anchoring isn't a member — that's a feature, not a bug (it's the antagonist, not part of the stack). The compression → transmission → distortion → rebuild pipeline is well-grounded.
